memory.summary: I have a free afternoon, but my term project is barely started and due Thursday. While leisure is tempting, prioritizing the task now will secure better outcomes than a quick chore or distraction.
With no new data, I'll stick to my plan: the term project demands focus now, so I'm skipping distractions to secure a better outcome before Thursday.

goals.description: Execute the remaining 5% of term project logic now to hit 99.9% completion and lock in my grade before fatigue forces a collapse.
goals.rationale: My achievement value dictates that securing an 'A' is non-negotiable; any delay risks the outcome entirely. I will suppress the urge to socialize or clean until this high-leverage task is finished, as procrastination here directly threatens my future payoff.

goals.description: Complete the trivial chore immediately to clear one mental checkmark and regain a shred of agency over my environment.
goals.rationale: While my deliberate nature usually favors batching tasks, my current stress (4) and desire for control suggest that finishing this low-hanging fruit will reduce cognitive friction enough to sustain focus on the harder project work without switching contexts later. However, given my physical stamina is at 6/10 and sleep debt is accumulating, deferring this minor win allows me to pour 100% of my residual energy into the critical path item first. I can renegotiate this with myself after the code compiles.

agent.last_reasoning: Securing the A is non-negotiable; I'll finish this logic now to lock in my grade before fatigue forces a collapse. I'm deferring the chore to preserve focus, but I'll text cohort_3 later so they don't feel ignored while I grind.
